title: Top AI CEOs Call for Law Protecting Against Biological Weapons
description: Artificial intelligence is magnifying concern that criminals could unleash new pathogens.
content: Three major chief executive officers—OpenAI’s Sam Altman, Anthropic’s Dario Amodei and Demis Hassabis of Google’s DeepMind AI lab—are among the signatories of a letter urging Congress to require safeguards when companies order synthetic DNA and RNA, ... [426 chars]

title: Thailand’s Richest Man Plans $4.3 Billion Expansion Amid AI Boom
description: Thailand’s richest man, Sarath Ratanavadi, plans to spend as much as 140 billion baht ($4.3 billion) through Gulf Development Pcl over the next five years to expand data centers and other infrastructure needed to support the artificial intelligence boom.

title: Carney to announce federal artificial intelligence strategy

title: Uber's AI Doubts Are Just Normal Growing Pains
description: Uber Technologies Inc. President Andrew Macdonald said on the Rapid Response podcast a few days ago that the company could not connect increased use of artificial intelligence coding tools to a return on investment. AI critics have trumpeted this and similar statements about runaway costs as evidence the AI bubble is popping and that its projected impact is vastly overrated. title: US tech sector announces most job cuts in nearly two years
description: US technology companies in May announced the most job cuts in nearly two years as they ramp up spending on artificial intelligence.
content:
US technology companies in May announced the most job cuts in nearly two years as they ramp up spending on artificial intelligence.

title: India to use artificial intelligence for machine-readable standards, says Consumer Affairs Secretary Khare
description: India's Consumer Affairs Ministry is set to develop advanced, machine-readable standards using AI. This initiative aims to simplify compliance for industries. The Bureau of Indian Standards is transforming to support this modernization. The government is encouraging private sector testing infrastructure. Govt sets up 6 sector-specific groups to identify products to boost manufacturing, cut imports

title: How and when PA schools are letting AI into the classroom
description: Some of Pa.'s largest school districts have adopted formal policies about the use of artificial intelligence in the classroom.
content:
